questions.question: The National eCourts Project, launched by the Department of Justice, is primarily aimed at:
questions.option_1: digitising and upgrading district and subordinate courts
questions.option_3: building new court complexes in rural areas
questions.option_4: establishing a nationwide telecom system for police

questions.question: India's highway network is one of the largest in the world. Which scheme focuses on the development of highways, particularly in economic corridors?
Which of the following schemes aims to skill rural youth who are poor and provide them with jobs having regular monthly wages or above the minimum wages?
questions.option_1: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)

questions.question: Which is the first State where India's first organic fish cluster was launched by the Central Government?
Which of the following infrastructure initiatives is associated with urban development in India?
questions.option_2: Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation
questions.option_3: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan

questions.question: The Mahila Kisan Sashaktikaran Pariyojana (MKSP), is a key sub-scheme under ____________.
questions.option_2: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
questions.option_3: Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
questions.option_4: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)

questions.question: Which Ministry is primarily responsible for implementing most rural development schemes in India?
questions.option_1: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
questions.option_2: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

questions.question: The Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) provides legal assurance of wage employment in rural areas. Under MGNREGA, how many days of guaranteed wage employment per year is a rural household entitled to?
Under the National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC), which mission specifically promotes the development of solar energy in India?
Which ministry releases the Performance Grading Index (PGI) 2.0 to assess the performance of states and union territories in school education?
questions.option_1: Ministry of Women and Child Development
questions.option_2: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
questions.option_4: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

questions.question: Which of the following government bodies is responsible for disaster response and management at the national level in India?
Digital literacy is now essential for human development in the knowledge economy. Which programme was launched to promote digital literacy among rural citizens in India?
Who among the following are eligible for the training programs under Deen Dayal Upadhyay – Gramin Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)?
questions.option_1: Individuals between the ages of 18 and 35 years
questions.option_2: Individuals between the ages of 15 and 35 years
questions.option_3: Individuals between the ages of 15 and 25 years
questions.option_4: Individuals between the ages of 25 and 55 years

questions.question: Which of the following is a government initiative aimed at improving urban infrastructure in Indian cities?
Financial inclusion helps reduce income inequality in rural areas. Which platform provides a unified digital database for unorganised workers?
Which initiative promotes rooftop solar installations for households?
Where is the primary focus of the Sampoorna Grameen Rozgar Yojana (SGRY)?
When was the Community Development Programme (CDP) launched in India?
Which of the following schemes focuses on providing rural connectivity through all-weather roads?
What is the primary aim of the National Urban Livelihoods Mission (NULM)?
questions.option_1: Providing subsidised housing to rural workers
questions.option_2: Encouraging foreign investment in urban infrastructure
questions.option_3: Promoting smart cities in Tier-2 towns
questions.option_4: Reducing urban poverty through skill development and self-employment

questions.question: The 'National Mission on High Yielding Seeds' aims to:

In which of the following years was the Swarnjayanti Gram Swarozgar Yojana (SGSY) launched with the aim to bring the assisted poor families (Swarozgaris) above the poverty line by organising them into Self Help Groups (SHGs) through a mix of bank credit and government subsidies?
Clean cooking fuel is essential for rural women’s health and environmental sustainability. Which scheme provides LPG connections to women in BPL households?
Which of the following groups is provided support under the One-Stop Centre (OSC) scheme?
questions.option_1: Only women working in government offices
questions.option_2: All women and girls below 18 years of age affected by violence
questions.option_3: Only women from a specific caste or religion
questions.option_4: Only married women in urban areas

questions.question: In which of the following years was the Bharatmala Pariyojana approved by the Government of India covering a length of 34,800 km to improve connectivity and reduce logistics cost in the country?
